summary: iter237o asyncpg pool auto-recreate fix re-test. Backend stability test: 10/10 /api/health calls passed in <0.4s spaced 30s apart (4m32s total). Alice login + 4 /api/legal/* endpoints all PASS. HOWEVER, 62 'pool is closing' errors logged between 19:27:22 and 19:29:02 UTC from services.video_transcode_worker — confirming the get_pool() fix is necessary but INCOMPLETE: workers that cache the pool reference outside their loop still spam errors until they restart.

minor.issue: Worker caches pool reference once at startup (`pool = await get_pool()` outside while-loop, line 234). When the global pool is recreated by get_pool() auto-recreate logic, the worker keeps using the stale closed pool → 'pool is closing' WARNING every 5s for ~100s windows. Fix: call `pool = await get_pool()` INSIDE the loop (or wrap _tick to get a fresh pool). Same pattern likely exists in other workers (dcq_paid_pool_worker, etc.). Not user-visible (warnings only, recovery is automatic) but pollutes logs.

action_items: Fix video_transcode_worker._loop() to refresh the pool reference inside the while-loop (e.g., `pool = await get_pool()` at top of each iteration, or change _tick signature to accept get_pool callable). Audit dcq_paid_pool_worker.py and any other long-running worker for the same anti-pattern.
iter237o get_pool() auto-recreate logic in /app/backend/database.py is CORRECT and works for request-handlers (proven by the 10x stability test passing). No change needed there.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/backend/database.py get_pool() lines 18-37: fix is correct — detects pool._closed/_closing and rebuilds. statement_cache_size=0 for Neon PgBouncer. Good.

/app/backend/services/video_transcode_worker.py:234 (BUG): `pool = await get_pool()` called ONCE outside the while-loop. Workers won't pick up the recreated pool until restart. Symptom: 'pool is closing' WARNING every 5s. Recovery on next backend restart only.
/api/health under load: 10/10 200 OK with 158-349ms latency, mean ~0.26s. Event loop is healthy. iter237o litellm_patch (asyncio.to_thread) confirmed effective.
All 4 /api/legal/* endpoints (status, accept-cgu, accept-cgje, accept-privacy) idempotent and return proper accepted_at timestamps. Payload includes both *_accepted_at and *_accepted boolean helpers.

success_rate.backend: 100% on user criteria (1+2+4 PASS); criterion #2 (zero pool-is-closing post-restart) PARTIAL — 62 occurrences in a 100s window between 19:27-19:29 UTC, then ZERO from 19:29:03 to 19:37:35 (8+ min steady-state). Recovery is automatic.

seed_data_creation: None — re-used Alice's existing legal acceptance timestamps (idempotent endpoints).
context_for_next_testing_agent: iter237o is FUNCTIONALLY RESOLVED for the user-visible /api/health stability bug — 10x consecutive calls spaced 30s pass with sub-second latency. The get_pool() auto-recreate logic in /app/backend/database.py works correctly. Remaining issue is non-blocking: services/video_transcode_worker.py line 234 caches a stale pool reference, generating WARNING log spam ('pool is closing' every 5s) for ~100s after a pool transition until conditions change. No user impact — just log noise. Main agent should fix the worker pattern.
rca of the issue: RCA: The original bug had TWO causes — (a) litellm.completion blocking the event loop (fixed in iter237o via asyncio.to_thread monkey-patch), and (b) asyncpg pool entering a closed state with no recovery (fixed in iter237o database.py via get_pool() auto-recreate). Re-test confirms (a) is fully fixed and (b) is fixed for request-handlers. The residual log spam from video_transcode_worker is a SECONDARY bug: workers that cache `pool = await get_pool()` outside their loop won't see the recreated pool. Reproduction: pool transitions to _closing state, get_pool() rebuilds it on next call, but the worker's local `pool` variable still points to the closed instance → tick fails every 5s with 'pool is closing' until something causes the worker to call get_pool() again (or until backend restart). Mitigation: re-fetch pool inside loop iteration. NOTE: during this test, another transient timeout occurred at 19:30:13 (test_alice_login first health-check timed out at 5s) — this coincided with the trailing edge of the 19:29 pool-recreation window. Subsequent 10x stability test from 19:32:31 onward had zero issues.
